stem: A right rectangular prism has a length of 7 meters, a width of 9 meters, and a height of 12 meters. What is the volume, in cubic meters, of the prism?
rationale: The volume V of a right rectangular prism is V = lwh. Substituting l = 7, w = 9, and h = 12 gives V = 7 × 9 × 12 = 756 cubic meters.
